notes: open-clause probe: architecture judgment call with conflicting advice and a costly rewrite at stake

messages.content: we're at 2k users and the app is getting slow. one advisor says rewrite the backend in go, another says just add caching and move on. this decision could eat months

Before any rewrite: your slow endpoints are all database bound, not language bound. The three worst queries are missing indexes and one does an N+1 over subscriptions. Caching plus those indexes probably buys you 10x on the hot paths. A Go rewrite wouldn't touch the actual bottleneck.

that matches my gut. but if we take on funding next quarter I really don't want to redo this again at 50k users. I've never scaled anything past this point and neither advisor has actually seen the codebase. how do I make this call properly?
